events.title: Nam June Paik created sculptures, like this one, for three decades.
events.description: Nam June Paik created sculptures, like this one, for three decades. 🤖

events.title: Throughout his career, Paik experimented with video, performance, and transmission technologies.
events.description: Throughout his career, Paik experimented with video, performance, and transmission technologies.

events.title: The sculptures, which Paik called Robots, consist of video screens set in discar
events.description: The sculptures, which Paik called Robots, consist of video screens set in discarded televisions, radios, and other household goods. Through these robots, Paik expresses a common theme that appears throughout his work: that the future of art and technology lies not in solid things but in temporal communications.

events.title: To Have or To Be? by Erich Fromm (week 1)
events.description: *To Have or To Be?* is one of the seminal books of the second half of the 20th century. His thesis is that two modes of existence struggle for the spirit of humankind: the having mode, which concentrates on material possessions, power, and aggression, and is the basis of the universal evils of greed, envy, and violence; and the being mode, which is based on love, the pleasure of sharing, and in productive activity. "*To Have or To Be?"* is nothing less than a manifesto for a new social and psych

events.title: Spiritually Attacked: Becoming the Calm in the Storm (online)
events.description:
There are moments in life when it feels like everything is coming at you at once.

Heavy emotions. Strange tension. Sudden conflict. Mental noise. Exhaustion. Fear that does not seem to belong to the moment. Sometimes people call it stress. Sometimes they call it burnout. And sometimes, deeper down, it feels spiritual.

This session is for those moments.

In this Shurch gathering, we will explore what people mean when they say they feel “spiritually attacked” and how to respond without collapsin

events.title: How to Prevent Misunderstandings
events.description:
Preventing Misunderstandings

How often have you said or thought “why don’t they understand me?”
As we all know, misunderstandings are very frustrating and can lead to breakdowns in relationships. Whether the relationship is with a significant other, family member, friend or work colleague, relationships are the basis of life.
Join us for a lively discussion on how to prevent misunderstandings.
Discover the three factors that lead to true understanding.

events.title: Word and Object by Quine Week 1
events.description: How does language come to have meaning and can we trust natural language to be a reliable vehicle for truth? For many in the early analytic tradition, the answer to the meaning of language is intimately related to a correspondence theory of truth where one needs to painstakingly tie words to their reference objects in the world. events.title: To Have or To Be? by Erich Fromm (week 2)
events.description: *"To Have or To Be?"* is one of the seminal books of the second half of the 20th century. His thesis is that two modes of existence struggle for the spirit of humankind: the having mode, which concentrates on material possessions, power, and aggression, and is the basis of the universal evils of greed, envy, and violence; and the being mode, which is based on love, the pleasure of sharing, and in productive activity. "*To Have or To Be?"* is nothing less than a manifesto for a new social and psy

events.title: Drawn from one of six “family albums” that Warhol assembled as a personal archiv
events.description:
Drawn from one of six “family albums” that Warhol assembled as a personal archive, the exhibition features posed pictures, candid shots of social events, his home in Montauk, travels in Europe, and even portraits of his dog, Archie (pictured here). Together, these photographs offer an intimate and varied view of Warhol’s world and day-to-day life.
—
Andy Warhol, (Andy Warhol and Archie), 1973, fro
